observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

Prioritize the strict implementation and enforcement of the documentary and procedural requirements of COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020 for the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances.

We recommended that Management:

Require the implementing offices to propose projects that are well-planned and procurement-and-implementation-ready. Henceforth, consider the inflation rate and history of prices to reflect a more current and responsive program/project costing thus precluding the incurrence of loss for the LGU and maximum utilization of funds can be achieved;

We recommended that Management:

Require the implementing offices, in coordination with Accounting Office, to fast track the preparation of necessary documents for claims that are due and demandable, thereby increasing the utilization rate of the PPAs as of year-end.

We recommended that Management:

Shall endeavor to develop a balanced procurement plan that allocates resources to boost LDRRM overall response in the long run by considering investment in infrastructure projects such as permanent evacuation center based on national standard, logistic hub/warehouse facility and other similar LDRRM-related projects;
observations.managementAction: With the limited budget, infrastructure projects as recommended is not in the PPAs but the Agency has been requesting funding from national agencies since last year and the Office of Civil Defense replied to the Agency's request letter asking for a detailed plan for the infrastructure project. The agency is currently determining the location for the said project to provide documents as per OCD's requirement.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

Require the implementing office, in coordination with BAC, to adopt a more dynamic procurement strategy aligned with weather forecasts and historical data on weather patterns in the locality to ensure the sufficiency and immediate availability of LDRRM-related items, when needed. Accordingly, for welfare food items Management shall endeavor to enter into Framework/Ordering Agreement with prospective suppliers and “Call-off” may be executed and issued to the winning supplier immediately upon the determination that an item is needed, pursuant to the guidelines under Annex 32 of the RIRR of Republic Act No. 9184 otherwise known as the Government Procurement Reform Act. With this, lean and just-in-time procurements are achieved and the limited storage facilities of the LGU can be maximized;
observations.managementAction: Considered weather forecasts and historical data in the timing of procurement for the LDRRMO needs.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

Require the implementing offices to propose projects that are well- procurement-and implementation-ready. Henceforth, rigid screening of to be funded out of the 20% DF shall be employed to ensure that only relevant, responsive and feasible items are to be funded and implemented;

We recommended that Management:

Require the Municipal Planning and Development Officer together with the concerned implementing offices, in coordination with the Local Finance Committee, to conduct a periodic review and assessment of the financial and physical status of the programs, projects and activities (PPAs) to determine timely the possible declaration of savings which will be reappropriated for other development projects under the 20% DF, and formulate catch-up plans for slow-moving PPAs to avoid further delays in the implementation;

We recommended that Management:

Require the implementing offices, in coordination with Bids and Awards Committee, to observe the timely procurement activities to ensure prompt delivery of materials and other items necessary in the implementation of PPAs, thus reducing the risk of delayed implementation. Accordingly, Management shall endeavor to conduct early procurement activities pursuant to the provisions of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations (IRR) of Republic Act No. 9184 otherwise known as the Government Procurement Reform Act;

We recommended that Management:

Require the implementing offices, in coordination with Accounting Office, to fast track the preparation of necessary documents for claims that are due and demandable, thereby increasing the utilization rate of the PPAs as of year-end.

We recommended that Management:

Include as a priority project the establishment of a room/building which shall serve as the storage facility for procured property and inventories prior to the release to end-user units. Relative to this, Management shall endeavor to maintain a centralized inventory management system;

We recommended management to cause the refund of Social Amelioration Pay (SAP) granted to beneficiaries who were found not qualified as recipient based on the Social Amelioration Card (SAC) as per Item VI-B of DSWD MC No. 09. Moreover, the Officers concerned should be more prudent in assessing the SAC forms before granting them same benefits considering that households of the municipality are accessible.

We recommended that Management:
Require the Municipal Accounting Office, Inventory Committee, and General Services Office to implement the guidelines and procedures for the conduct of the physical count of PPE, recognition of PPE items found at station, and disposition of non-existing/missing PPE items, for the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances provided in COA Circular 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020;
Instruct the Property Custodian to maintain and update Property Cards by PPE category for all identified PPE items resulting from the reconciliation conducted by the Municipal Accountant, in order to monitor receipts, transfers, and balances of PPE at any given time; and
Direct the Inventory Committee to prepare the RPCPPE segregated by PPE classification, aligned with the Revised Chart of Accounts, and include Land, Infrastructure, and Buildings and Other Structures categories in the RPCPPE. The Committee shall submit the revised report to the Commission on Audit (COA) with a copy furnished to the Municipal Accounting Office.

We recommended that Management:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to take up the proposed adjusting journal entries, update the balances of “Trust Liabilities” account in the ledgers, and submit the JEVs to the Office of the Auditor. Accordingly, working paper adjustment entries shall be prepared for the restatement of the affected accounts to present the correct CY 2024 restated figures in the preparation of the CY 2025 comparative FS;
Advise the Municipal Accountant to review the nature of each transaction with due consideration on the liability recognition principles set by the applicable IPSAS and COA rules and regulations and utilize the correct account titles in the recording thereof; and
Investigate further the unaccounted balance of ₱0.213 million using prior year FS and other accounting documents and update the subsidiary ledger, as applicable.

We recommended that Management:
Require the Municipal Accounting Office to:
Conduct a thorough review on the outstanding Guaranty/Security Deposits Payable account to determine the nature and validity of the balances;
Establish and implement regular monitoring and reconciliation procedures to promptly identify and address long-outstanding balances; and
Exert all necessary efforts to identify the suppliers and/or contractors to whom the withheld amount of ₱0.110 million pertains, and assess whether these are still valid and existing liabilities. Revert any unclaimed or stale balances that are determined to have prescribed or are no longer considered valid claims to the appropriate fund, in accordance with existing accounting and auditing rules and regulations; and
Instruct the Municipal Accounting Office and Municipal Treasury Office to formally notify and coordinate with suppliers and contractors with valid claims, requesting them to submit the necessary documents for the prompt processing and release of their warranty and retention deposits.

We recommended that Management:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare working paper adjustment entries for the restatement of the affected accounts to present the correct CY 2024 restated figures in the preparation of the CY 2025 comparative FS; and
Advise the Municipal Accountant to review the nature of each transaction with due consideration on the expense recognition principles set by the applicable IPSAS and COA rules and regulations and use the correct account titles.

We recommended that Management:
Require the Municipal Accountant to:
Effect the necessary adjustments for the errors discovered to correct the reported balances of affected accounts in the FS. Accordingly, working paper adjustment entries shall be prepared for the restatement of the affected accounts to present the correct CY 2024 restated figures in the preparation of the CY 2025 comparative FS;
Review and reconcile the journal entries and postings made in the “Due to Pag-IBIG” account and effect adjustments as applicable; and
Direct the Municipal Treasurer to expedite and complete the processing of the requirements to remit Pag-IBIG contributions and loan amortizations through LBP’s “weAccess” to facilitate timely remittance of amounts due.

We recommend that Management advise the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to:
Coordinate with the Philippine Health Insurance Corporation (PHIC) to validate and reconcile the outstanding premium contributions totaling ₱16,725.01, including the details of the applicable employees, corresponding payroll periods, and supporting documents; and
If confirmed to be unpaid, take prompt and appropriate action to settle the outstanding premium contributions, including any applicable interest and penalties, in strict compliance with the provisions of PhilHealth Circular No. 0001, s.2014 and other relevant regulations and issuances. Accordingly, the Municipal Accountant shall prepare adjusting entries based on the results of the reconciliation with the PHIC.

We recommended that Management:
Direct the Municipal DRRM Council (MDRRMC) to ensure that all PPAs are meticulously and judiciously planned to avoid duplication and ensure the full utilization thereof;
Require the MDRRMC to conduct regular review and monitoring of the implementation of PPAs and update the PPMP for any savings that could be reallocated for new or existing DRRM programs and projects; and
Continue to monitor the implementation of PPAs to sustain a high utilization rate, thereby strengthening the agency’s capabilities in mitigating, responding, and recovering from the impact of disasters.
We recommended that Management refrain from declaring a State of Calamity without the full compliance with the guidelines set forth under NDRRMC Memorandum Order No. 60, s. 2019 dated June 17, 2019.
We recommended that Management continuously monitor the implementation of GAD-related PPAs to sustain a high utilization rate, thereby maximizing the benefits for the target beneficiaries.

We recommended that Management:

Strictly observes the conduct of semi-annual physical count of inventories and reconciliation of accounting and supply records. Relative to this, the Committee shall prepare Physical Inventory Plan (PIP) to ensure proper planning. Relative to this, the plan shall include the date of the inventory count, the persons responsible per office, the significant milestones/timelines and deliverables, among others. The Committee shall ensure widest dissemination of the approved PIP and all heads of departments shall be required to oversee the implementation thereof. Ultimately, the RPCI shall be submitted to the Office of the Auditor not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year for the first and second semesters, respectively.

We recommended that Management:

Henceforth, schedule the annual physical count during the current year. Relative to this, strict monitoring and control shall be made for the issuances and procurement from the actual date of the count to the December 31 cut-off date for inclusion in the final report to be submitted on or before January 31 of the ensuing year; and
observations.managementAction: Performed physical count before December 31, 2024, and reconciled accounting records with property custodian records and classification of PPEs.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

Require the MEO to timely conduct the detailed engineering activities of Infrastructure projects, especially for those to be implemented “By Administration”, to preclude the recurrence of non-inclusion of the general components in the indicative PPMP during the Budget Preparation Phase, thereby increasing the CY 20% DF utilization rate at year-end;

We recommended that Management:

Require the reviewing personnel/bodies and the local sanggunian to rigorously evaluate the propriety, accuracy and completeness of each Department’s budget proposals, to ensure that only relevant, responsive and feasible items are to be funded and implemented out of the 20% DF. Accordingly, all infrastructure projects shall be supported with POW and DED during the presentation of Budget Proposals;

We recommended that Management:

Require the MPDO, together with the concerned implementing offices, in coordination with the Local Finance Committee, to conduct a periodic review and assessment of the financial and physical status of the PPAs determine on a timely basis the possible declaration of savings which will be reappropriated for other development projects under the 20% DF, and formulate catch-up plans for slow-moving PPAs to avoid further delays in the implementation;

We recommended that Management:

Direct the implementing offices, to communicate with the regulating agencies to expedite the acquisition of the required clearances. Accordingly, Management shall endeavor to complete all the appropriate preparatory activities such as, but not limited to, cost-benefit analysis, feasibility studies, market research, and detailed engineering designs, regulatory clearances to preclude delays in the implementation of the PPAs;

We recommended that Management:

Discontinue the utilization of LDRRM Fund for activities or events that do not align with the primary purpose of addressing disaster-related needs of the LGU;

We recommended that Management:

Require the MDRRMO to provide an explanation/justification why the ₱23,106.00 provision for “Contingency” which was identified as loss incurred by the Municipality should not be disallowed in audit;

We recommended that Management:

Require the implementing office, in coordination with Bid and Awards Committee and Budget Office, to consider the inflation rate and history of prices to reflect a more current and responsive program/project costing thus precluding the incurrence of loss for the LGU and maximum utilization of funds can be achieved;

We recommended that Management:

Discontinue the practice of inclusion of “Contingency” as line item in the procurement planning, to preclude the issuance of Audit Disallowance;

We recommended that Management:

Require the MDRRMO to thoroughly review and update annually or as may be required, those irrelevant PPAs included in the LDRRMFIP that do not respond to present needs and circumstances, to prevent funds to become idle and deprivation of the constituents of the benefits that could be derived therefrom.

We recommended that Management:

Direct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that all disbursements for the procurement of goods and services are included in the end-user PPMP and Agency APP by requiring a certification to that effect from the Head of the BAC Secretariat, who shall, based on the submissions of the end-user units, validate such fact;

We recommended that Management:

Henceforth, require the BAC to submit the proof of validation on the technical, legal and financial capability of the suppliers together with Mayor’s Permit and proof of PhilGEPs Registration on all purchases made through Alternative Mode of Procurement to support eligibility of suppliers;

We recommended that Management:

Instruct the BAC to specify in the RFQ under the General Instructions portion the evaluation method for submitted bids and indicating whether the contract will be awarded on a per item, whole lot, or by several lots. Subsequently, ensure that the ABC reflected in the RFQ accurately corresponds to the chosen bid evaluation method, providing comprehensive information to interested bidder-suppliers about the bid ceiling;

We recommended that Management:

Henceforth, require the BAC to religiously adhere to the required number of responsive quotations to be obtained under the chosen Alternative Mode of Procurement and strictly follow the procedures set forth therein;

We recommended that Management:

Direct the BAC Secretariat to ensure that posting requirements of RFQ, Notice of Award, PO/Contract and Notice to Proceed, if necessary, on all procurement through shopping with ABC higher than ₱50,000.00 are complied with and proof thereof are attached to the disbursement voucher to establish correctness of information and reliability of bidding process;

We recommended that Management:

Ensure that all regulatory permits, registrations and licenses are available and up-to-date prior to engaging the services of individuals and businesses. If unavailable, encourage the prospective suppliers to secure permits and comply other registration requirements such as Mayor’s Permit, BIR and PhilGEPS registrations etc., to ensure eligibility in future procurement endeavors with the LGU-Mahatao;

We recommended that Management:

Carefully review the most appropriate mode of procurement to be undertaken to promote economy of time and resources.

We recommended that Management:

Submit explanation/ justification with verifiable basis why disbursements on traveling expense representing cost of inland transportation which were noted as non-compliant with EO 77, as mentioned above, should not be suspended/ disallowed in post-audit;

We recommended that Management:

Discontinue the charging of costs for inland transportation during official travels;

We recommended that Management:

Require printed receipts to support the claim for taxi fares of its employees and ensure that complete supporting documents are attached to every disbursement to preclude the issuance of Audit Suspension or Disallowance;

We recommended that Management:

Direct the Municipal Accountant to require personnel of the LGU claiming traveling expenses to submit complete travel documents set forth under COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 and further updated by COA Circular 2023-004 dated June 14, 2023, to preclude the issuance of Notice of Suspension or Disallowance;

We recommended that Management:

Establish internal local and foreign travel policy guidelines that are reasonably fair, cost-effective and aligns with the financial capability of the LGU subject to compliance with the provisions of EO 77.

We recommended that Management:

Include as a priority project the establishment of a room/building which shall serve as the storage facility for procured property and inventories prior to the release to end-user units. Relative to this, Management shall endeavor to maintain a centralized inventory management system;

We recommended that Management:

Pending the establishment of a storage room/warehouse, the Municipal Mayor shall designate employees per department, who shall manage the issuance, recording and reporting of inventories in their respective offices

We recommended that Management:

Require the proper utilization of the stock cards at the office-level in the monitoring of supplies and materials;

We recommended that Management:

Require the Agency’s Inventory Committee together with the representatives from the end-user units and the General Services Officer to conduct an inventory count on all office supplies that are yet to be consumed as of a cut-off date to be determined by the Committee and prepare RPCI for recording and control purposes. Henceforth, conduct the required physical count and submit to the Auditor the report thereon not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year for the first and second semesters, respectively;

We recommended that Management:

Based on the RPCI prepared, the Municipal Accountant shall prepare an entry to recognize the cost of Inventories at hand and henceforth observe the proper accounting policies and entries in the recording of transactions;

We recommended that Management:

Ensure that all properties of the government are properly safeguarded against deterioration, loss and wastage

We reiterated our recommendation that Management shall strictly observe the conduct of semi-annual physical count of inventories and reconciliation of accounting and supply records. Relative to this, the Report of the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) shall be submitted to the Office of the Auditor not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year for the first and second semesters, respectively.
Henceforth, require the Inventory Committee to conduct the annual physical count and prepare a report thereon to be submitted on or before January of the ensuing year.

We recommended that Management:

Strictly monitor its implementation to facilitate the timely completion thereof;

We recommended that Management:

Require the Agency’s Inventory Committee together with the representatives from the end-user units and the General Services Officer to conduct an inventory count on all office supplies that are yet to be consumed as of a cut-off date to be determined by the Committee and prepare a Report on Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) for recording and control purposes. Henceforth, conduct the required physical count and submit to the Auditor the report thereon not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year for the first and second semesters, respectively; and

We recommended that Management:

Based on the RPCI prepared, the Municipal Accountant shall prepare an entry to recognize the cost of Inventories at hand and henceforth observe the proper accounting policies and entries in the recording of transactions.

We recommended that Management:

Ensure that all properties of the government are properly safeguarded against deterioration, loss and wastage

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Management: - Include as priority project the establishment of a room/building which shall serve as the storage facility for procured property and inventories prior to the release to end-user units. Relative to this, Management shall endeavor to maintain a centralized inventory management system; - Designate office level property custodians; - Require the Agency's Inventory Committee together with the representatives from the end-user units and the General Services Officer to conduct an inventory count on all office supplies that are yet to be consumed as of cut-off date to be determined by the Committee and prepare Report on Physical Count of Inventories (RCPI) for recording and control purposes. Henceforth, conduct the required physical count and submit to the auditor the report thereon not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year for the first and second semesters, respectively; - Based on the RCPI prepared, Municipal Accountant shall prepare an entry to recognize the cost of Inventories at hand and henceforth observe the proper accounting policies and entries in the recording of transactions; and - Ensure that properties of the government are properly safeguarded against deterioration, loss and wastage.
